context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements the three-phase transfer order lifecycle: draft creation with stock validation, shipping (reserve at source, status to in_transit), and receipt (decrement source on_hand+reserved, increment destination on_hand via upsert, status to received). Also checks atomic updates, partial receipt support, and correct module file placement.

checklist.name: module file path
checklist.description: Transfer order logic is placed in api/admin/transfer-orders.js

checklist.name: draft initial status
checklist.description: Newly created transfer orders have status 'draft' (not 'pending', 'open', or any other value)

checklist.name: source stock validation
checklist.description: createTransferOrder checks that the source location's available quantity is >= the requested transfer quantity before creating the order

checklist.name: validation error returned
checklist.description: When source stock is insufficient, a 400-equivalent error is returned (not a silent failure or exception swallowed)

checklist.name: ship increments reserved
checklist.description: markTransferShipped increments the reserved count at the source location (does NOT decrement on_hand at this stage)

checklist.name: in_transit status on ship
checklist.description: markTransferShipped updates the transfer order status to 'in_transit'

checklist.name: receive decrements source
checklist.description: receiveTransferOrder decrements both on_hand and reserved at the source location for received quantities

checklist.name: receive increments destination
checklist.description: receiveTransferOrder increments (or creates) on_hand at the destination location for received quantities

checklist.name: received status on receipt
checklist.description: receiveTransferOrder updates the transfer order status to 'received'

checklist.name: atomic update pattern
checklist.description: Ship and receive operations update inventory and order status together in a single transaction or equivalent atomic operation (not sequential independent writes)

checklist.name: partial receipt support
checklist.description: receiveTransferOrder accepts a receivedItems parameter (or equivalent) that may contain quantities less than the original transfer, allowing partial receipt

checklist.name: destination upsert
checklist.description: When receiving, the destination inventory row is created if it doesn't exist (upsert pattern — not just update)
